 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ba0w","title":"P6.4: Security Audit Checklist","description":"# P6.4: Security Audit Checklist

## Goal
Systematically review all security-sensitive code paths against a comprehensive checklist, documenting findings and ensuring no known vulnerability patterns exist in the implementation.

## Background & Rationale

### Why Formal Security Audit
1. **Cryptographic Code**: Small mistakes have catastrophic consequences
2. **Web Security**: XSS, injection, and other web vulnerabilities
3. **Trust Boundaries**: Data flows between trusted and untrusted contexts
4. **Defense in Depth**: Multiple security layers must all be correct

### Audit Scope
- Rust cryptographic implementation
- JavaScript browser crypto code
- Web viewer UI and input handling
- Service worker and caching
- Generated documentation and configuration

## Audit Checklist

### 1. Cryptographic Implementation

#### Key Derivation
- [ ] Argon2id parameters meet minimum security (m≥64MB, t≥3, p≥4)
- [ ] Salt is unique per archive (not reused)
- [ ] Salt length is at least 16 bytes
- [ ] Password is properly encoded (UTF-8 normalized)
- [ ] Memory is zeroed after use (where possible)

#### AES-GCM Encryption
- [ ] 256-bit keys used (not 128 or 192)
- [ ] Nonces are never reused with same key
- [ ] Nonce generation is counter-based or random with collision resistance
- [ ] Authentication tags are verified before any processing
- [ ] Tag length is 128 bits (not truncated)
- [ ] AAD binds ciphertext to context

#### Key Management
- [ ] DEK is generated with CSPRNG
- [ ] DEK is never stored in plaintext
- [ ] KEK derivation uses separate salt/context
- [ ] Key slots are independent (compromising one doesnt leak others)
- [ ] No key material in logs or error messages

#### HKDF Usage
- [ ] Proper salt handling (can be empty, but documented)
- [ ] Context/info parameter differentiates key uses
- [ ] Output length matches algorithm requirements

### 2. Web Security

#### Input Handling
- [ ] All user input is validated/sanitized
- [ ] No innerHTML with user content
- [ ] Query parameters are escaped before display
- [ ] Form inputs have appropriate types
- [ ] File uploads are validated (if any)

#### Content Security Policy
- [ ] CSP header is set and restrictive
- [ ] No unsafe-inline for scripts
- [ ] No unsafe-eval
- [ ] No data: URLs for scripts
- [ ] frame-ancestors restricts embedding

#### Cross-Origin Security
- [ ] CORS headers are minimal/absent (static site)
- [ ] COOP: same-origin is set
- [ ] COEP: require-corp is set
- [ ] No sensitive data in URLs

#### Authentication
- [ ] Password entry clears on navigation
- [ ] Decrypted data not cached in localStorage
- [ ] Session timeout implemented
- [ ] Failed attempts dont leak timing info

### 3. Data Handling

#### Sensitive Data
- [ ] Passwords cleared from memory after use
- [ ] Decrypted content not persisted to disk
- [ ] No sensitive data in console.log
- [ ] Error messages dont leak content
- [ ] Browser autofill disabled for password

#### Export Process
- [ ] Secret scan runs before export
- [ ] User confirms understanding of risks
- [ ] No accidental plaintext copies
- [ ] Temporary files are securely deleted

### 4. Service Worker Security

#### Caching
- [ ] Only static assets cached (not decrypted data)
- [ ] Cache invalidation on update
- [ ] No credential caching
- [ ] Fetch interception doesnt leak data

#### Installation
- [ ] Update prompts user to refresh
- [ ] Old versions are properly cleaned up
- [ ] No downgrade attacks possible

### 5. Build and Distribution

#### Dependencies
- [ ] All dependencies audited (cargo audit, npm audit)
- [ ] No known vulnerable versions
- [ ] Lockfile committed and verified
- [ ] Minimal dependency surface

#### Supply Chain
- [ ] Build is reproducible
- [ ] Release artifacts are signed
- [ ] No post-install scripts with network access
- [ ] Subresource integrity for CDN resources (if any)

### 6. Code Quality

#### Error Handling
- [ ] Crypto errors dont reveal key material
- [ ] Decryption failures are indistinguishable
- [ ] Panics dont leak sensitive state
- [ ] All error paths tested

#### Timing Attacks
- [ ] Password comparison is constant-time
- [ ] Tag verification is constant-time
- [ ] No early exit on partial match
- [ ] Benchmarks dont reveal timing

### 7. Documentation

#### Security Documentation
- [ ] Threat model is documented
- [ ] Limitations are clearly stated
- [ ] Key rotation procedures documented
- [ ] Incident response guidance

#### User Guidance
- [ ] Password strength requirements explained
- [ ] Recovery procedures documented
- [ ] Public hosting risks explained
- [ ] Key backup importance emphasized

## Audit Procedure

### For Each Checklist Item

```markdown
## Item: [Checklist item description]

**Status:** PASS / FAIL / N/A

**Evidence:**
[Link to code, test, or documentation]

**Finding:**
[Description of finding if any]

**Remediation:**
[Required fix if FAIL]

**Reviewer:** [Name]
**Date:** [YYYY-MM-DD]
```

### Audit Report Template

```markdown
# CASS Security Audit Report

## Summary
- Total items: 47
- Passed: 45
- Failed: 1
- N/A: 1

## Critical Findings
[None / List]

## High Findings
[None / List]

## Medium Findings
[List with remediation status]

## Low Findings
[List with notes]

## Recommendations
[Future improvements]
```

## Files to Create

- `docs/SECURITY_AUDIT_CHECKLIST.md`: Full checklist
- `docs/SECURITY_AUDIT_REPORT.md`: Audit results
- `scripts/security_scan.sh`: Automated checks
- `tests/security/`: Security-focused tests
- `.github/workflows/security.yml`: Automated security CI

## Exit Criteria
- [ ] All checklist items reviewed
- [ ] No critical or high severity findings
- [ ] All medium findings have remediation plan
- [ ] Audit report published
- [ ] Automated checks integrated in CI
- [ ] Third-party dependencies audited","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T01:48:33.545333Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T17:21:24.766740Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T17:21:24.766740Z","close_reason":"Completed security audit: Fixed HIGH-001 XOR nonce derivation issue in both Rust and JS. Created comprehensive 56-item audit checklist and detailed report. Added 7 security tests verifying nonce uniqueness. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ba0w","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h0uc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-bbhj","title":"TST.SRC: E2E Tests for Sources Mappings Commands","description":"# Task: Add E2E Tests for Sources Mappings Subcommands

## Context
The `cass sources mappings` subcommands (P6.3) need E2E test coverage. These commands manage path rewriting rules.

## Current Test Status
`tests/e2e_sources.rs` covers add/list/remove/doctor/sync but NOT mappings subcommands.

## Tests to Add

### cass sources mappings list
1. `test_mappings_list_empty` - No mappings configured
2. `test_mappings_list_with_mappings` - Show configured mappings
3. `test_mappings_list_json` - JSON output format
4. `test_mappings_list_nonexistent_source` - Error for unknown source

### cass sources mappings add
1. `test_mappings_add_basic` - Add simple mapping
2. `test_mappings_add_with_agents` - Add with agent filter
3. `test_mappings_add_multiple` - Add multiple mappings
4. `test_mappings_add_to_nonexistent_source` - Error handling

### cass sources mappings remove
1. `test_mappings_remove_by_index` - Remove by index
2. `test_mappings_remove_invalid_index` - Error for out-of-range
3. `test_mappings_remove_from_empty` - Error when no mappings

### cass sources mappings test
1. `test_mappings_test_match` - Path matches a mapping
2. `test_mappings_test_no_match` - Path doesn't match
3. `test_mappings_test_with_agent` - Test with agent filter

## Implementation
Add tests to `tests/e2e_sources.rs` in new section for mappings.

## Technical Notes
- Use same pattern as existing sources tests
- Create temp config with source first
- Then test mappings subcommands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:58:39.898215Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:27:54.643848Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:27:54.643848Z","close_reason":"Added 15 E2E tests for sources mappings commands: list (4 tests), add (4 tests), remove (3 tests), test (3 tests), plus 1 workflow integration test. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-bbhj","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h2i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-be51l","title":"[MEDIUM] health: top-level db omits open_skipped truth flag","description":"Recent health skip-open work sets state.database.open_skipped=true, but run_health only copies exists/opened/conversations/messages/open_error/counts_skipped into the top-level db object and response_schema_health_db also omits open_skipped. Robot clients reading health --json .db.opened cannot distinguish assumed-good opened=true from a real DB-open success unless they know to inspect .state.database.open_skipped.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T02:48:58.662850162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T02:48:58.662850162Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Robot clients reading health --json .db.opened cannot distinguish assumed-good opened=true from a real DB-open success unless they know to inspect .state.database.open_skipped.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T02:48:58.662850162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T16:03:22.216606647Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T16:03:22.216157156Z","close_reason":"Expose health db.open_skipped in payload and schema with skip/probe regressions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-be7","title":"OpenCode Connector Tests (Actual Implementation)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedRiver","created_at":"2025-12-17T05:44:13.428431Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T05:46:57.766017Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T05:46:57.766017Z","close_reason":"Closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-bfk","title":"Phase 2: Plumb Provenance into Indexing Pipeline","description":"# Phase 2: Plumb Provenance into Indexing Pipeline

## Overview
Now that storage supports provenance, we need to wire it through the indexing pipeline so every conversation gets proper origin metadata.

## Key Changes

### 1. Extend ScanContext
Current ScanContext only has data_root and since_ts. We need:
- List of scan roots (not just one)
- Per-root provenance information
- Platform hints for path mapping

### 2. Indexer Multi-Root Support
The indexer should:
- Build list of scan roots (local defaults + remote mirrors)
- For each root, track which source it belongs to
- Inject provenance into normalized conversations

### 3. Connector Changes (Minimal)
Connectors should NOT need to know about provenance. The indexer:
- Calls connector.scan() with a scan root
- Receives NormalizedConversations
- Injects provenance before persistence

### 4. Deduplication Fix
Current dedup erases origin distinctions. After this phase:
- Dedup key includes source_id
- Same content from different sources = distinct results

## Dependencies
- Phase 1 complete (storage supports provenance)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] ScanContext supports multiple roots with provenance
- [ ] Indexer injects provenance for each root
- [ ] Connectors unchanged (provenance injected externally)
- [ ] Local source works exactly as before
- [ ] Dedup respects source boundaries","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:56:44.348978Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:33:46.035565Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:33:46.035565Z","close_reason":"All Phase 2 sub-tasks complete: P2.1 (ScanContext), P2.2 (indexer multi-root), P2.3 (dedup fix). Provenance now plumbed through indexing pipeline.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-bfk","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-c8e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-bgi","title":"Phase 5: Remote Sources Configuration & Sync Engine","description":"# Phase 5: Remote Sources Configuration & Sync Engine

## Overview
This is the centerpiece of the remote sources feature. It adds:
1. Configuration system for defining remote sources
2. Sync engine that pulls sessions from remote machines via SSH/rsync
3. CLI commands for managing sources (add, list, sync, doctor)

## Goals
1. `cass sources add ssh://user@host` to register a remote source
2. `cass sources list` to show configured sources with sync status
3. `cass sources sync` to pull latest sessions from all remotes
4. `cass sources doctor` to diagnose connectivity/permission issues

## Technical Approach
- Config stored in `~/.config/cass/sources.toml` or similar
- Sync uses rsync over SSH for efficiency (delta transfer)
- Remote agent session directories are synced to `~/.local/share/cass/remotes/{hostname}/`
- After sync, normal indexing pipeline picks up the sessions with provenance set

## Config Format Example
```toml
[[sources]]
name = "laptop"
type = "ssh"
host = "user@laptop.local"
paths = [
  "~/.claude/projects",
  "~/.cursor",
]
sync_schedule = "manual"  # or "hourly", "daily"

[[sources]]
name = "workstation"
type = "ssh"
host = "user@work.example.com"
paths = ["~/.claude/projects"]
```

## Dependencies
- Requires Phase 2 completion (indexer knows about provenance)
- Independent of Phases 3-4 (can run in parallel)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cass sources add` validates SSH connectivity before saving
- [ ] `cass sources sync` uses rsync with progress indication
- [ ] Partial sync failures don't corrupt existing data
- [ ] `cass sources doctor` reports common issues with remediation hints
- [ ] Synced sessions appear in search with correct source attribution","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T06:01:07.269280Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:18:49.738865Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:18:49.738865Z","close_reason":"All Phase 5 tasks complete: P5.1 (config structs), P5.2 (sources add), P5.3 (sources list), P5.4 (sync engine), P5.5 (sources sync), P5.6 (sources doctor), P5.7 (sources remove). Full remote sources configuration and sync capability implemented.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-bgi","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-bfk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
